readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Clever Mind of the Octopus
paragraphs: Of all the creatures in the sea, few are as surprising as the octopus. With a soft, boneless body and eight curling arms, it looks nothing like the animals we usually call intelligent. Yet scientists who study it have come to see it as one of the cleverest creatures in the ocean, capable of solving problems that would puzzle many a larger animal.

Much of its talent lies in those eight arms. Each arm is lined with hundreds of sensitive suckers that can taste as well as touch, and a large share of the animal's nerve cells are spread through the arms rather than packed inside its head. In a sense, an octopus does not simply think with its brain; it also thinks, in part, with its limbs, which can explore and react almost on their own.

This unusual body gives the octopus remarkable skills. In laboratory tanks, individuals have learned to open screw-top jars to reach the food inside, and some have slipped out of their tanks at night to raid a neighbour's supper before returning unseen. Such behaviour hints at curiosity and memory, qualities we rarely expect to find in an animal so distant from ourselves on the tree of life.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Despite its strange body, the octopus is a remarkably intelligent animal that can learn and solve problems.
The octopus is a simple creature whose behaviour shows no sign of memory or curiosity.
Octopuses are dangerous animals that scientists are afraid to keep in laboratory tanks.
The intelligence of the octopus comes entirely from its brain and not from its arms.

questions.before: The octopus is unusual because many of its nerve cells are found in its arms rather than

questions.prompt: オクトパスが賢い行動をとった具体例を示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: a soft, boneless body and eight curling arms
hundreds of sensitive suckers that can taste as well as touch
learned to open screw-top jars to reach the food inside
an animal so distant from ourselves on the tree of life

questions.sentence: In other words, its intelligence seems to be spread throughout its body rather than gathered in one place.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で述べられている内容として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: In experiments, octopuses have opened jars and even escaped their tanks, showing curiosity and memory.
Octopuses in laboratories refuse to eat and never try to leave their tanks.
Scientists have found that octopuses cannot remember anything for more than a few seconds.
Octopuses build their own tanks at night and share food with their neighbours.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: How Tea Travelled the World
paragraphs: Today tea is one of the most popular drinks on the planet, yet its story began thousands of years ago in the hills of southern China. According to an old legend, a few wild leaves once drifted into a pot of boiling water, giving off such a pleasant smell that people decided to drink the result. Whether or not the tale is true, tea soon became part of daily life across the region.

For many centuries, the secret of growing and preparing tea stayed within China. Merchants carried dried leaves along busy trade routes, and the drink slowly reached neighbouring lands, where it took on new customs and flavours. In some places it was served plain and hot; in others, people added milk, spices, or sugar to suit their own taste.

When European traders arrived by sea, tea began its longest journey of all. Ships carried heavy chests of leaves across the oceans, and the drink became fashionable in distant cities far from where it grew. In time, tea was no longer the product of a single country but a shared pleasure enjoyed in countless forms around the world.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Tea began in China and gradually spread around the world, taking on new customs along the way.
Tea was invented in Europe and later carried by ships to China for the first time.
Tea has always been served in exactly the same way in every country.
Tea was never traded between countries and stayed only in southern China.

questions.before: For many centuries, the knowledge of how to grow and prepare tea

questions.prompt: 国によって茶の楽しみ方が違ったことを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: a few wild leaves once drifted into a pot of boiling water
Merchants carried dried leaves along busy trade routes
people added milk, spices, or sugar to suit their own taste
Ships carried heavy chests of leaves across the oceans

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: From its beginnings in China, tea spread through trade and sea travel to become a shared drink enjoyed worldwide.
Tea remained a secret known only to a few merchants and was never enjoyed outside China.
European ships destroyed the tea trade and stopped the drink from spreading any further.
Tea became popular only because every country prepared it in exactly one fixed way.
